why: Round 1 cold spec-judge returned ALIGNED false on the builder lens: six leaked rubric nouns (bare threshold/rubric) in the Then/And steps of four of the seven authored scenarios, which the untagged-scenario boolean-form ban rejects. I authored those scenarios and never ran the bar's own mechanical form check over them before submitting — the check is self-run by the producer per the producer bar, and settling it costs no judge round. Reworded to the cut vocabulary the surrounding prose already uses; check-suite re-run clean and the edit class re-confirmed additive. Round 2 cold judge returned ALIGNED true on all three lenses.

cause: wrong worked example in authored prose
why: The first draft of the correction section illustrated the impossible case with stripping one max:2 dimension out of [2,3,2,2] threshold:7 and claimed the survivors reach 5. Checked the example against the rule it illustrates before submitting: stripping one leaves 7, which is the zero-slack case, not the impossible one — reaching 5 takes stripping two. Replaced with [3,3,2] threshold:6 strip max:3 leaves 5 against 6, verified arithmetically, and kept [2,3,2] threshold:5 as the distinct zero-slack example. Both examples are drawn from shapes that occur in the real corpus rather than constructed.

cause: brief premise stale and issue reasoning unsound
why: Two of the brief's load-bearing premises did not survive measurement. The issue's ~19 rubrics is stale: the corpus holds 37 real rubrics counted by threshold lines, of which cyberfleet holds 12. And the issue's stated reason for the Clearance routing — each is a mixed edit, so each routes to Clearance — is unsound: measured against the real classifier, the natural in-scenario correction shape reports narrowing, not mixed. The routing conclusion holds because narrowing and mixed both route to Clearance; the reason does not, and encoding mixed as the handle would let a producer read not-mixed and conclude self-clear.

cause: authored Givens reused the artifact's worked examples
why: The cold impl-judge passed all seven scenarios but escalated rather than asserting on whether one scenario's Given had absorbed the governance's worked example, declaring itself unable to classify. Its escalation was right and understated: it exempted the neighbouring example on provenance, reasoning that a pre-existing illustration cannot have been absorbed because causality runs the other way. That is the wrong axis. The test-vector rule bars a Given from reusing the existing artifact's worked examples, and pre-existing is precisely what existing means, so both scenarios reused apparatus a copier could echo back without reasoning. Rewrote the three digit-bearing Givens to express the relation instead of an instance — a removal leaving the survivors unable to reach the unchanged cut, and one leaving them totaling exactly it. The scenarios now state the boundary rather than an instance of it, no worked example is echoable, and the edit stays additive.
